data.label: Above horizon
data.description: The body is located above the local horizon for the chart time and location.
data.runtime_usage: Boolean low-level condition used for local visibility, diurnal sect checks, visible hemisphere classification, hayz rules and accidental dignity logic.
data.calculation_notes: Prefer direct altitude or a robust Ascendant/Descendant horizon calculation over a house-only shortcut. A positive altitude or configured positive horizon delta maps to this condition.

data.label: Below horizon
data.description: The body is located below the local horizon for the chart time and location.
data.runtime_usage: Boolean low-level condition used for local invisibility, nocturnal sect checks, invisible hemisphere classification, hayz rules and accidental dignity logic.
data.calculation_notes: Prefer direct altitude or a robust Ascendant/Descendant horizon calculation over a house-only shortcut. A negative altitude or configured negative horizon delta maps to this condition.

data.label: On horizon
data.description: The body is on or very close to the local horizon within a configured technical tolerance.
data.runtime_usage: Boundary condition for objects near the Ascendant or Descendant where strict above/below classification would be arbitrary without an explicit threshold.
data.calculation_notes: Use a configurable horizon_delta_degrees tolerance. This condition should be selected when the absolute altitude or horizon delta is inside that tolerance.
